differential_diagnoses: Echis ocellatus envenomation with systemic envenomation (hemotoxic)
Acute kidney injury (likely acute tubular necrosis or cortical necrosis)
next_steps: Identify the snake species (likely Echis ocellatus) and administer appropriate antivenom
Initiate fluid resuscitation and blood product replacement (fresh frozen plasma, platelets, packed red blood cells)
Obstetric consultation for monitoring of fetal distress and assessment for placental abruption
Urgent transfer to a facility with dialysis capabilities for management of acute renal failure
Monitor hematological and renal function parameters closely
Avoid unnecessary invasive procedures prior to correction of coagulopathy

differential_diagnoses: Echis ocellatus envenomation with systemic coagulopathy
Placental abruption secondary to venom-induced consumption coagulopathy
Acute kidney injury (acute tubular necrosis and likely cortical necrosis)
next_steps: Remove tourniquet with immediate hemodynamic monitoring
Administration of appropriate polyvalent snake antivenom
Blood product transfusion (fresh frozen plasma, platelets, and packed red blood cells) to correct coagulopathy
Bedside ultrasound for placental localization and fetal status assessment
Foley catheter insertion for hourly urine output monitoring
Obstetric team activation for potential emergency cesarean section
Stabilization and urgent transfer to a facility capable of hemodialysis and critical care
